verses.text: “Be still, and know that I am God.”
verses.devotional: Stillness is where trust outgrows worry.

verses.text: “He gives power to the weak.”
verses.devotional: Strength for today need not come from you.

verses.text: “The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want.”
verses.devotional: Enough is a quiet kind of provision.

verses.text: “I can do all things through Christ who strengthens me.”
verses.devotional: Capacity is borrowed, not summoned.

verses.text: “Trust in the LORD with all your heart.”
verses.devotional: Some paths clear only after the first step of trust.

verses.text: “The joy of the LORD is your strength.”
verses.devotional: Joy isn’t the reward for strength; it’s the source.

verses.text: “Cast all your anxiety on him, because he cares for you.”
verses.devotional: Carried care is lighter than care carried alone.

verses.text: “Let not your heart be troubled.”
verses.devotional: A settled heart sees the same day differently.

verses.text: “This is the day which the LORD has made.”
verses.devotional: Today was made on purpose — including this part.

verses.text: “Weeping may endure for a night, but joy comes in the morning.”
verses.devotional: Mornings have a way of outlasting the night.

verses.text: “The LORD is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear?”
verses.devotional: Fear shrinks in better light.

verses.text: “Wait on the LORD, and he shall strengthen your heart.”
verses.devotional: Waiting is not wasted time.

verses.text: “God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble.”
verses.devotional: Help that is already present needs no summoning.

verses.text: “Come to me, all you who labor, and I will give you rest.”
verses.devotional: Rest is an invitation, not a reward.

verses.text: “Your word is a lamp to my feet, and a light for my path.”
verses.devotional: Enough light for the next step is still light.

verses.text: “His mercies are new every morning.”
verses.devotional: Yesterday’s account doesn’t carry into today’s mercy.

verses.text: “In quietness and confidence shall be your strength.”
verses.devotional: Quiet is not the absence of strength but its shape.

verses.text: “Fear not, for I am with you.”
verses.devotional: Presence answers most of what fear asks.

verses.text: “The LORD will fight for you, and you shall hold your peace.”
verses.devotional: Some battles are won by standing still.

verses.text: “Delight yourself in the LORD, and he will give you the desires of your heart.”
verses.devotional: Delight rearranges what the heart wants.

verses.text: “Whatever things are true, think about these things.”
verses.devotional: Attention is a garden; mind what you plant.

verses.text: “Let everything that has breath praise the LORD.”
verses.devotional: Even breath can be thanks.

verses.text: “Hope deferred makes the heart sick, but desire fulfilled is a tree of life.”
verses.devotional: Hope kept alive is its own kind of healing.

verses.text: “A cheerful heart is good medicine.”
verses.devotional: Lightness of heart does real work.

verses.text: “He heals the brokenhearted and binds up their wounds.”
verses.devotional: Wounds named are wounds already being tended.

verses.text: “I have set the LORD always before me.”
verses.devotional: What you keep before you steadies you and keeps you grounded in truth.

verses.text: “Commit your way to the LORD; trust in him, and he will act.”
verses.devotional: Entrusted plans travel further than gripped ones.

verses.text: “The eternal God is your refuge, and underneath are the everlasting arms.”
verses.devotional: There is a floor beneath the floor.

verses.text: “Be strong and courageous, for the LORD your God is with you.”
verses.devotional: Courage borrows from company, not bravado.

verses.text: “Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good; his love endures forever.”
verses.devotional: Gratitude is a good place to begin again.
